name: How do I phase a kitchen remodel in an occupied home in Pacifica or San Bruno?

mainEntity.name: If I can’t move out, how can a contractor phase a kitchen remodel in a Pacifica or San Bruno home so my family still has basic cooking and cleaning access?
mainEntity.text: If I can’t move out, how can a contractor phase a kitchen remodel in a Pacifica or San Bruno home so my family still has basic cooking and cleaning access?

acceptedAnswer.text: Remodeling a kitchen while living in the home is common in Pacifica and San Bruno, but it requires deliberate planning with your contractor. Before demolition, discuss a clear phasing plan that outlines which days utilities will be off, which parts of the room will be accessible, and where a temporary kitchen can be created. A typical temporary kitchen includes relocating the refrigerator, a microwave or hot plate, and possibly a toaster oven to a nearby room, along with folding tables for prep. Many contractors can provide or hook up a temporary sink using an existing laundry or bathroom connection so you maintain access to running water. Ask the contractor to group the noisiest and dustiest work—demolition, drywall sanding, floor removal—into compact blocks and to seal off the work zone with plastic and zipper doors. Daily cleanup expectations should be written into the contract, including vacuuming pathways and removing debris. Clarify work hours, weekend work policies, and how pets and children will be safely separated from the jobsite. A good phasing plan minimizes disruption while still allowing the crew to work efficiently, and it should be finalized before the project starts so you can plan meals, grocery runs, and potential short hotel stays if necessary.
acceptedAnswer.description: When you must stay in the home, ask your contractor to phase work and set up a temporary kitchen. Common approaches include relocating a fridge and microwave, providing a temporary sink hookup, scheduling loud or dusty work in blocks, and maintaining clear access paths. A detailed phasing plan should be agreed on before demolition.
